subject: UX & Research
title: The Macintosh and Mass Adoption
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Macintosh and Mass Adoption.

hook.text: On January 24, 1984, Steve Jobs unveiled the Macintosh. It said 'Hello' from the screen. The crowd went wild.

buildup.text: The Mac brought the GUI to consumers: icons, folders, a trash can, and a one-button mouse. No command line needed.

discovery.text: Apple published the Human Interface Guidelines. For the first time, software had consistent rules for menus and dialogs.

twist.text: The first Mac was too expensive at $2,495 and had only 128KB RAM. Sales disappointed until prices dropped.

climax.text: The Mac proved that ordinary people could use computers. Desktop publishing, later killer apps, drove real adoption.

punchline.text: The Mac made computing human. The GUI stopped being research and became reality.

quiz.question: What was the Macintosh's key contribution to HCI?
options: Bringing the graphical user interface to mass-market consumers
